task: Improve readability and naming only. Do not change outputs.
source_code:
def normalize_email(value):
    value = value.strip()
    value = value.lower()
    if "+" in value:
        local, domain = value.split("@")
        local = local.split("+")[0]
        value = local + "@" + domain
    return value
expected_property: Same normalization results

task: Refactor and clean up. Do not change outputs or edge-case behavior.
source_code:
class Slugifier
  def self.slugify(text)
    text = text.downcase.strip
    text = text.gsub(" ", "-")
    text = text.gsub(/[^a-z0-9\-]/, "")
    text
  end
end
tests:
require_relative "source"
require "minitest/autorun"

class SlugifierTest < Minitest::Test
  def test_basic
    assert_equal "hello-world", Slugifier.slugify("Hello World")
  end

  def test_strip
    assert_equal "hello", Slugifier.slugify("  Hello  ")
  end

  def test_symbols_removed
    assert_equal "hello-world", Slugifier.slugify("Hello @ World!")
  end
end
expected_property: Same slug strings

task: Refactor this function while preserving exact return values.
source_code:
<?php
function status_label($active, $verified) {
    if (!$active) {
        return "inactive";
    }
    if ($verified) {
        return "active_verified";
    }
    return "active_pending";
}
tests:
<?php
use PHPUnit\Framework\TestCase;

final class StatusLabelTest extends TestCase {
    public function testInactive() {
        $this->assertSame("inactive", status_label(false, false));
    }

    public function testVerified() {
        $this->assertSame("active_verified", status_label(true, true));
    }

    public function testPending() {
        $this->assertSame("active_pending", status_label(true, false));
    }
}
expected_property: Same label mapping

task: Refactor this method using Ruby idioms. Tests must pass unchanged.
source_code:
class Cart
  def total_with_tax(items, tax_rate)
    subtotal = 0
    items.each do |item|
      subtotal = subtotal + item[:price] * item[:qty]
    end
    subtotal + (subtotal * tax_rate).round(2)
  end
end
tests:
require_relative 'source'
require 'minitest/autorun'

class CartTest < Minitest::Test
  def test_simple
    items = [{price: 10.0, qty: 2}, {price: 5.0, qty: 1}]
    assert_equal 27.5, Cart.new.total_with_tax(items, 0.1)
  end

  def test_zero_tax
    items = [{price: 20.0, qty: 1}]
    assert_equal 20.0, Cart.new.total_with_tax(items, 0)
  end

  def test_empty
    assert_equal 0, Cart.new.total_with_tax([], 0.2)
  end
end
expected_property: Same total including tax for all inputs

task: Refactor this function for clarity. PHPUnit tests must pass.
source_code:
<?php
function group_by_key(array $records, string $key): array {
    $groups = [];
    foreach ($records as $r) {
        $val = $r[$key] ?? 'unknown';
        if (!isset($groups[$val])) {
            $groups[$val] = [];
        }
        $groups[$val][] = $r;
    }
    return $groups;
}
tests:
<?php
use PHPUnit\Framework\TestCase;

final class GroupByKeyTest extends TestCase {
    public function testGroups() {
        $data = [
            ['status' => 'new', 'id' => 1],
            ['status' => 'done', 'id' => 2],
            ['status' => 'new', 'id' => 3],
        ];
        $result = group_by_key($data, 'status');
        $this->assertCount(2, $result['new']);
        $this->assertCount(1, $result['done']);
    }

    public function testMissingKey() {
        $data = [['id' => 1]];
        $result = group_by_key($data, 'status');
        $this->assertArrayHasKey('unknown', $result);
    }
}
expected_property: Same grouping structure and fallback behavior

task: Replace the loop with functional methods. Tests must pass unchanged.
source_code:
class Paginator
  def pages(total, per_page)
    result = []
    page = 1
    offset = 0
    while offset < total
      result << { page: page, offset: offset }
      page += 1
      offset += per_page
    end
    result
  end
end
tests:
require_relative 'source'
require 'minitest/autorun'

class PaginatorTest < Minitest::Test
  def test_exact_pages
    assert_equal [
      {page: 1, offset: 0},
      {page: 2, offset: 10}
    ], Paginator.new.pages(20, 10)
  end

  def test_partial_last_page
    result = Paginator.new.pages(25, 10)
    assert_equal 3, result.length
    assert_equal 20, result.last[:offset]
  end

  def test_empty
    assert_equal [], Paginator.new.pages(0, 10)
  end
end
expected_property: Same page/offset pairs for all inputs
